name: Gollizno Loop – Subbetic Mountains
description:
Granada is much more than the Sierra Nevada and the Gollizno Route, which begins and ends in the beautiful town of Moclín, is good proof of this. This itinerary takes you to explore the closure of the Velillos River, where you have to cross a suspension bridge not suitable for those with a lot of vertigo. The surroundings are incredible and also hide a wall with cave paintings. In addition, Moclín and its castle are well worth a visit.

This circular route is short, but quite intense, because most of the way is uphill. Of course, you can choose to do the route in the opposite direction; You will have fewer kilometers of climbing, but the last part will be harder. Bring trekking poles, as they will be of great help.

The best times to enjoy this adventure are, without a doubt, spring, autumn and winter. Due to the heat, it is best to avoid summer. Moclín is very close to Granada and has all the services you may need. To get there, the most comfortable thing is to use your own vehicle. If you don't have one, there are buses from Granada.
region_intro: Hiking around Moclín offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, deep gorges, and historical features. The region, situated in the Subbetic Mountains, features limestone formations, olive groves, and river valleys. Trails often lead through varied terrain, providing views of the surrounding countryside and the historic Moclín castle. Best hiking trails around Moclín The most popular hiking route is Gollizno Route Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This path explores the dramatic Gollizno gorge with its unique rock formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gollizno Loop – Subbetic Mountains, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.2 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Subbetic Mountains and the Moclín area. Local hikers also love the Cañón de Los Cahorros – Gollizno Suspension Bridge loop from Moclín, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through a canyon and featuring a suspension bridge, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Hiking around Moclín is defined by its gorges, olive groves, and historical sites, including the Moclín castle. The network offers options for easy family friendly hikes as well as more challenging hiking trails Moclín. The routes in Moclín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Moclín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Moclín?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails available around Moclín, catering to various skill levels. The region offers a diverse range of routes, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Moclín's hiking trails?
A: Moclín's hiking trails feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills, deep gorges, and historical sites. You'll encounter limestone formations, olive groves, and river valleys, with many paths offering views of the surrounding Subbetic Mountains and the historic Moclín castle.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Moclín?
A: Yes, Moclín offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351408778/caparacena-trail-sl-a-305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caparacena Trail (SL-A 305)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1-mile (6.7 km) route. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19348995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Los Cahorros – Gollizno Suspension Bridge loop from Moclín&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.5 miles (4.1 km) and features a suspension bridge, making it an engaging walk for all ages.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Moclín?
A: Yes, Moclín also provides challenging routes for more experienced hikers. While the majority are moderate, there are 5 difficult trails to explore. These routes often involve greater elevation changes and longer distances, providing a more strenuous experience within the Subbetic Mountains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Moclín?
A: Yes, Moclín features several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497798073/gollizno-loop-subbetic-mountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Loop – Subbetic Mountains&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.5-mile (7.2 km) path offering panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21834369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony – Viewpoint of Moclín loop from Moclín&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.7 miles (5.9 km) and includes a notable viewpoint.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Moclín?
A: Many trails in Moclín are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near protected natural habitats. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, as water sources can be limited on some routes.

Q: Are there hiking trails with good viewpoints in Moclín?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21834369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony – Viewpoint of Moclín loop from Moclín&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint. Many routes, especially those in the Subbetic Mountains, offer panoramic views of the olive groves and surrounding countryside.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I see while hiking in Moclín?
A: Moclín is rich in history. Many trails offer views of or pass by significant landmarks. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a central feature. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony&lt;/a&gt;, or explore nearby historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6092781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water elements like rivers or gorges?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by its dramatic gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977651655/gollizno-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, explores the impressive Gollizno gorge with its unique rock formations and river passages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19348995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Los Cahorros – Gollizno Suspension Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; also takes you through a canyon with water features.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Moclín?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages and popular trailheads in the Moclín area. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for precise parking information, especially for less frequented trails.

Q: How long do hikes in Moclín typically take?
A: Hike durations in Moclín vary widely depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19348995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Los Cahorros – Gollizno Suspension Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes, while longer moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977651655/gollizno-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Route Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours 5 minutes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Moclín?
A: The trails in Moclín are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the historical elements like the castle, and the well-maintained paths through gorges and olive groves.
tips: Moclín was the last frontier of the Nasrid kingdom of Granada and the protagonist of one of the decisive battles in which King Boabdil lost the Alhambra and went into exile in North Africa. Its citadel or castle is one of the most significant monuments of the town, but by no means the only one.
From the hermitage of the Virgen de las Angustias you have a very good perspective of the town of Olivares and a large part of the west of Granada.
The Gollizno route in Granada: viewpoints and suspension bridges over Tajos de la Hoz.

waypoints.name: View of Olivares from the Hermitage of Our Lady of Sorrows
